assessor_raw: Assessment of the Learning-to-Rank Marketplace Search experiment design. The experiment is well-structured with a chronological temporal split appropriate for non-stationary search logs. Label construction uses pairwise implicit feedback with position-bias mitigation via exclusion of positions 1-2 from training. Purchase-weighted labels (grade 2) add signal for conversion intent and the weight is a tuned hyperparameter. Both models receive seller re-ranking post-processing and equivalent tuning. NDCG@10 is an appropriate primary metric; secondary metrics including IPW-adjusted NDCG, tail query NDCG, and seller quality lift adequately address confounds. Confound controls (stratified analysis, exposure adjustment, cross-seller stratification) are comprehensive. Scope statement appropriately limits claims to offline evaluation. No methodological flaws identified. Verdict: defense_wins.
